summary: Stage decomp PR for Big Blue Route that replaced many raw Ground offset accesses with typed GroundVars and RouteEntry accesses, added a large C body for grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20, improved or matched several grbigblueroute functions, and updated route-related struct/header types. The only review-like evidence is the decomp bot report; no human review comments were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Main stage TU changes: typed field conversions, assert/report cleanup, function progress, grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 implementation, matching-tactic comments, and local include additions.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c added 439 deleted 127 hunks 22
diff: grbigblueroute.c raw pointer offsets replaced with gp->u.car and gp->gv.bigblueroute2 fields
diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 changed from placeholder comment to C implementation
bot_report: 4 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/gr/grbigblueroute

key_files.role: Header/type surface updates for RouteEntry and grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 signature.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.h added 2 deleted 2 hunks 2
diff: RouteEntry pad_24[4] changed to f32 x24
diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 prototype changed from UNK_RET to void

key_files.role: GroundVars type recovery for Big Blue Route car data and track data.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/types.h added 16 deleted 2 hunks 4
diff: grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars xC8 renamed to car_info
diff: added grBigBlueRoute_Track and grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars2
diff: Ground union arm changed from bigblueroute to bigblueroute2 and added u.car

what_changed: Replaced many raw casts and byte-offset accesses into Ground with typed accesses such as gp->u.car.car_info, gp->u.car.xCC/xD0/xD4, gp->gv.bigblueroute2.xC8, and gp->gv.bigblueroute2.tracks[i].offset/jobj.
Added or used route entry typing for the dynamically allocated car_info array, including RouteEntry flags and fields in grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 and grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C.

Added the C implementation of grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 with a do-while loop over route entries, switch cases for route state, spline/vector math, atan2f rotation, item/fighter proximity logic, and RE_ENTRY macro scoped around the function.
Converted some generated assert/report forms to project forms, including __assert to HSD_ASSERT and OSReport filename argument to __FILE__.
Replaced several extern float/s16 data-symbol uses with literals or known project data, including 30.0f, 4000.0f, 20.0f, 250.0f, -3.0f, and stage_info.x6DC.
Updated RouteEntry x24 from padding to f32 and changed grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 prototype to void.
Added includes for baselib/debug.h, lb/lbshadow.h, and trigf.h.

smart_moves.text: Typed GroundVars and RouteEntry accesses made repeated offset math reviewable while preserving the array/stride structure visible in the generated code.
evidence_refs: diff: *(HSD_JObj**) ((u8*) gp + 0xE4) replaced with gp->gv.bigblueroute2.tracks[0].jobj
diff: ((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i].flags.b0 replaces byte/stride flag reads

smart_moves.text: Function progress was documented with precise remaining-diff comments instead of claiming full matches for still-unmatched work.
evidence_refs: diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020BC68 todo updated to 99.83% with anonymous string-pool note
diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 todo notes 89.41% and systemic float/register-pressure differences
diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C todo notes register allocation and RouteEntry addressing-mode choices

smart_moves.text: Header signature was updated after recovering the body return type for grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20.
evidence_refs: diff: grbigblueroute.h changes grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 from UNK_RET to void

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a stage TU has repeated byte-offset access into Ground-owned state, recovering a local GroundVars arm and small element structs can turn pointer arithmetic into meaningful indexed fields without losing the loop/stride shape.
evidence_refs: diff: added grBigBlueRoute_Track and grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars2
diff: tracks[i].offset/jobj replaces gp + 0xD8/0xE4 style accesses
diff: RouteEntry array accesses replace gp->gv.bigblueroute.xC8 byte math

decomp_lessons.lesson: A partially matched large function can still be useful if the PR records narrow remaining causes such as float-constant hoisting, register pressure, and stack spill differences.
evidence_refs: diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 todo says 89.41% and names float-constant hoisting order and register pressure
bot_report: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 improved from 0.00% to 89.73%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use project assert/report forms when evidence supports them, but keep real reporting behavior such as OSReport calls.
evidence_refs: diff: __assert(__FILE__, 674, "gp->u.car.car_info") replaced by HSD_ASSERT(0x2A2, gp->u.car.car_info)
diff: OSReport call retained while filename argument changes to __FILE__

naming_conventions.name: car_info
naming_conventions.context: Renamed grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars xC8 to car_info and used it as the RouteEntry allocation/array backing storage.
evidence_refs: diff: types.h xC8 void* renamed to car_info
diff: gp->u.car.car_info = HSD_MemAlloc(0x554)
diff: ((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i]

naming_conventions.name: grBigBlueRoute_Track
naming_conventions.context: Introduced to name a repeated Vec3 offset plus HSD_JObj* pair used for route track jobjs.
evidence_refs: diff: types.h adds struct grBigBlueRoute_Track with Vec3 offset and HSD_JObj* jobj
diff: gp->gv.bigblueroute2.tracks[0..3]

naming_conventions.name: bigblueroute2
naming_conventions.context: Added as a separate GroundVars arm for the track-oriented layout used by some Big Blue Route functions.
evidence_refs: diff: Ground union arm changed to struct grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars2 bigblueroute2
diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020BC68 and grBigBlueRoute_8020BF38 use gp->gv.bigblueroute2

naming_conventions.name: RE_ENTRY
naming_conventions.context: Scoped helper macro for repeated RouteEntry indexing in grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20; undefed after the function.
evidence_refs: diff: #define RE_ENTRY (&((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i])
diff: #undef RE_ENTRY after grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural indexed loops replaced pointer-increment offset access for track and route-entry arrays.
evidence_refs: diff: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) over gp->gv.bigblueroute2.tracks[i]
diff: for (i = 0; i < 30; i++) over ((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(8) remained in grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C, with the function still documented as only partially improved.
evidence_refs: diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C keeps PAD_STACK(8)
diff: todo says 92.06% and names register allocation/addressing-mode choices

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Control-flow comparison was reshaped from >= to negated < in grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C, apparently to affect branch/codegen shape.
evidence_refs: diff: if ((f32) gp->gv.bigblueroute.x108 >= ...) changed to if (!((f32) gp->u.car.x108 < ...))

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Literal constants replaced some generated extern data symbols, but the bot reported a small .sdata regression, so this should be treated as PR-specific evidence rather than a blanket data-style rule.

diff: grBb_Route_804DB994/998/99C replaced with 20.0f, 250.0f, -3.0f

notes: Diff evidence is strong for file changes and code patterns, but raw_diff and raw_pr_json were loaded as truncated files in the prompt.
No PR body explanation or human review comments were available, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not inferred.
Bot report provides match/improvement evidence for specific symbols and sections, but no local build command, objdiff transcript, or final post-merge validation log is included in the slice.
Some in-code @todo percentages differ slightly from the bot report percentages, likely due to different moments in the PR; both are reported only as evidence from their respective sources.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover GroundVars and element structs before preserving byte-offset Ground access
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grbigblueroute, replacing gp byte offsets with GroundVars arms and RouteEntry/grBigBlueRoute_Track structs made repeated per-track and per-route-entry code more reviewable and coincided with multiple new matches or improvements.
evidence_refs: diff: added grBigBlueRoute_Track and grBigBlueRoute_GroundVars2
diff: gp->gv.bigblueroute2.tracks[i] and gp->u.car.car_info conversions
bot_report: grBigBlueRoute_8020BC68, grBigBlueRoute_8020BF38, grBigBlueRoute_8020C530 newly matched

accepted_candidate_records.title: Document remaining codegen blockers for large partial matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: For grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20, the PR included the recovered C body and explicitly noted remaining float-constant hoisting and register-pressure differences instead of presenting the function as complete.
evidence_refs: diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 @todo notes 89.41% and named blockers
bot_report: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 improved from 0.00% to 89.73%

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize literal replacement when section regressions appear
source_update_candidates.text: This PR replaced several extern float symbols with literals and improved text matches, but the bot also reported a broken .sdata match. Future guidance may mention checking data-section impact when replacing generated extern constants with literals.
evidence_refs: diff: extern f32 grBb_Route_804DB948/94C and grBb_Route_804DB994/998/99C removed in favor of literals
bot_report: .sdata broken match from 100.00% to 92.86%

source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate nuance for existing literals/data standards; not promoted because the slice lacks author/reviewer rationale and final section-tradeoff discussion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that RE_ENTRY-style macros are generally accepted style; the PR only shows a local macro used in one partial function and no review discussion.
Do not infer that PAD_STACK(8) is validated as necessary; it remained in a still-unmatched function without a verifier transcript in the slice.
Do not infer that replacing all generated extern constants with literals is globally safe; the bot reported a .sdata regression in this PR.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale from the merged state; no human review comments were loaded.
